title: phelypaea ramosa subsp. mutelii
intro: Phelypaea ramosa subsp. mutelii, commonly known as the purple mullein or mullein, is a perennial plant species native to the Iberian Peninsula. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Phelypaea ramosa subsp. mutelii include relief from respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as skin conditions like eczema and acne. The plant is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and poultices, to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
